occupation: Chairman at Indian Outdoor Advertising Association (IOAA)
headline: CEO & Board Leader | Turnaround & Institution Builder | Chairman – IOAA
summary:
I am a CEO and board-level leader with over three decades of experience leading complex, asset-heavy and infrastructure-linked businesses through periods of disruption, turnaround, and sustainable growth.
I currently serve as Chief Executive Officer of Times Innovative Media Limited (Times OOH), part of the Times Group. Following a severe business disruption during the pandemic, the organisation delivered a multi-year recovery significantly ahead of industry benchmarks, restoring profitability, strengthening cash flows, improving organisational productivity, and reducing portfolio concentration risk.
My leadership approach combines decisive crisis management with long-term structural thinking. Across roles, I have focused on restoring financial discipline, strengthening governance, rebuilding organisational culture, and creating scalable platforms for growth. I have led large teams through difficult transitions, taken tough but necessary decisions, and worked closely with boards, regulators, investors, and partners in high-stakes environments.
Prior to Times OOH, I held senior leadership roles at Alghanim Industries (Kuwait), Bharti Airtel, Bharti Retail, and Coca-Cola India. At Coca-Cola, I was part of the core team behind the iconic ₹5 pricing strategy that reshaped the category nationally. My career has spanned Media, Infrastructure-linked platforms, Telecom, FMCG, Retail, and diversified conglomerates across India and international markets.
In addition to my executive responsibilities, I am the elected Chairman of the Indian Outdoor Advertisers Association (IOAA) and have served on the boards of multiple operating and investment entities within a large diversified group, primarily in nominee and executive-director capacities. I actively engage in industry dialogue and have been invited to speak at national and international forums including GoaFest, the Outdoor Advertising Convention, and the World Out of Home Organization.

experiences.title: Chief Executive Officer
experiences.description: As the President of Times Innovative Media Limited, I was entrusted with the responsibility of driving growth, profitability, and purpose for the company as we emerged from the challenges of Covid-19. My role involved rebuilding team morale, providing strategic oversight and leadership across various facets of the organization, and ensuring alignment with our core values and long-term objectives. Key responsibilities included: Growth and Profitability: Spearheaded initiatives aimed at increasing revenue and enhancing profitability, leveraging my experience in strategic planning and operational efficiency. Strategic Leadership: Provided strategic direction to various departments, including HR and Business Development, ensuring cohesive and effective management. Organizational Restructuring: Restructured the organization and repurposed jobs to improve employee productivity and morale. Business Expansion: Led the expansion into new markets and sectors through strategically important contracts and by derisking the business from overdependence on specific revenue segments. Process Improvement and Automation: Championed IT-led process automation initiatives to enhance operational efficiency and customer relationship management. Board Directorships: Served on the Boards of several subsidiaries and associated entities Stakeholder Management: Cultivated and maintained strong relationships with key stakeholders, including employees, clients, agencies, property owners, investors, and shareholders, to support and drive the company's strategic goals. My tenure as President was marked by a blend of strategic foresight, operational excellence, and a deep commitment to fostering relationships and driving sustainable growth. This experience laid the groundwork for my current role as CEO, where I continue to build on these foundations to enhance value for all stakeholders.

experiences.title: Chief Financial Officer
experiences.description: As the Chief Financial Officer of the company, I was responsible for the comprehensive oversight of Finance, Legal, Business Development, Strategy and new contract acquisitions, I have had oversight of operations and Government Business too for a few years. My tenure was marked by a commitment to strategic leadership aimed at maximizing revenue and profitability. Key achievements and responsibilities included: 1. Strategic Leadership and Growth: I identified the Business to Government (B2G) vertical as a critical growth area, expanding its contribution from 1% to 20% of total revenues over four years. 2. Profitability Turnaround: By reorganizing the entire property portfolio and renegotiating contracts, I successfully turned the business profitable. This was driven by a relentless focus on cost optimization through productivity measures and effective performance management systems. 3. Acquisition Initiatives: I spearheaded our acquisition initiatives, securing marquee properties with exclusive marketing rights. These acquisitions significantly enhanced our market presence and revenue streams. 4. Process Automation and IT Integration: We became the first company in the group to implement an effective Customer Relationship Management software (Salesforce.com) and integrated it with SAP, driving IT-led process automation under my watch 5. Stakeholder Relationships: I grew and fostered long-term relationships with key stakeholders, including agencies, clients, property owners, investors, and shareholders, ensuring alignment and support for our strategic objectives. My tenure as CFO was characterized by a blend of strategic foresight, operational efficiency, and a strong focus on building and maintaining robust stakeholder relationships.

experiences.title: Finance Director
experiences.description: Key respnsibilities included: 1) Steered initiatives towards Strategic Planning, Corporate Governance & Internal Controls to enhance the value for the company and for the customer. 2) Implemented an impactful Performance Management System for the Electronics Retail group. 3) Led Transformation projects like Hyperion Consolidation, Hackett Benchmarking, SAP implementation Alghanim Industries is a $2B conglomerate headquartered at Kuwait and with varied business interest such as Retailing (Electronics, Home Furnishing, Automobile), Engineering, Manufacturing (Kirby building Systems), Shipping, Lighting, Transport and Travel)

experiences.title: Chief Controller - Bharti Airtel Ltd; Chief Financial Officer - Bharti Retail Ltd
experiences.description: 1) Had Oversight of Strategic Planning, Financial Reporting, Performance Review, Revenue Assurance, Risk & Compliance Management 2) Member of the 6 member Airtel Finance Council at the Apex level and a member of the Enterprise Business Management Board 3) Restructured the Finance Organisation for the B2B business during merger of Corporate business and Carrier Business into one Business Segment 4) Was an active member of core team involved in the launch of “International Calling Card” from US to India. 5) As CFO of Bharti Retail Limited was actively involved in the implementation of the Retail initiative of Bharti group with Wal-Mart. Bharti Enterprises Ltd is one of India’s leading business groups with interests in telecom, agri business, financial services, retail and manufacturing. Bharti has been a pioneering force in the telecom sector with many firsts and innovations to its credit. Bharti Airtel, a group company, is a leading global telecommunications company with operations in 18 countries across Asia and Africa.  Enterprise Services (ES) business vertical had 2 sub-verticals. ES-Corporates was in the B2B space providing consolidated telecom solutions to corporates. ES-Carriers owned the cabling infrastructure in the domestic and international space enabling the telecom 'carriage' for the rest of the group as well as for other telecom companies

experiences.title: General Manager-Finance
experiences.description: Held various seniors jobs in the organisation in in positions of increasing responsibilities, the last job being responsible for the Finance function of Nepal, Sri Lanka & Bangladesh. Key Highlights: Key Responsibilities included Financial Reporting in USGAAP and Indian GAAP, Monthly Management Information, Audit Compliance, Supply Chain & Logistics, Revenue Assurance, Risk Management Business Support through evaluation of new opportunities and product launches and real-time competition benchmarking Led the biggest (22% of Volume) and the most profitable region in the country. (2000-2004) Ideated and launched the ‘5 rupee Strategy’ while lead managing financial feasibility to the idea (2002-03). This was subsequently rolled out country wide leading to 80% growth in volumes and India Division winning the prestigious “Woodruff Cup” in 2002 Assisting Bottlers and Contract packers to set up capacity in record time (5 months) during the ‘Rs.5/- strategy’ roll out (2002) Restructuring business of South Zone by merging AP with the rest of the south states(2005) Implemented significant cost savings in concentrate procurement, for SWA region, by reworking the supply and logistics (2006)

experiences.title: Manager-Finance
experiences.description: 1) Set up an Internal Audit System to enhance the internal controls of Commercial and Non Commercial Activities. 2) Raised an ECB ($3 M) to fund strategic initiatives 3) Effective maintenance of books of accounts, payroll, Personal and Corporate Taxation, Costing and Budgeting. 4) Led initiatives towards Internal Audit and Systems review through study internal control system, developing specifications for computerisation of the bill passing system, Spares Parts Requirement Planning model for the Service network.

experiences.title: Deputy Manager
experiences.description: Complete Responsibility of Finance and Accounts at Hosur Factory. Financial Reporting and Business Planning and Analysis

experiences.description: Worked in the Automobile Cost Section analyzing cost sheets and Reconciliation of Inputs to Outputs of Raw Materials. Supplier Payments processing

recommendations:
HEMANT MEHTA CPA (Canada), FCCA (UK), FCA (India), CTP( USA)

Although I reported to Shekhar for a short period to time but that experience was truly enjoyable. I have never ever seen a person with so much positive energy, spirit and motivation. He is not only a good coach but also a great mentor. He sense of humor and presence of mind makes him a comfortable person to work along.  I'll recommend him anytime for a leadership role.

SL Narayanan

Shekhar is a complete finance professional.
 
I had the pleasure of working very closely with him in the early days of Bharti's foray into retailing. Shekhar brought great insights into the framing of accounting policies and indeed many of the operational decisions thanks to his earlier FMCG experience. He had a great quality of keeping a cool head even while chasing some impossible deadlines. Shekhar's work style was characterized by a collegial and enabling approach, always leading by personal example and inspired a bunch of subordinates that rose to the occasion every time we grappled the 'next big thing'. His understanding of linkages between finance and other functions such as IT, pricing, supply chain management, personnel etc were key to a successful execution of the project.
 
I have the highest regard for his expertise and will recommend Shekhar for any CFO job in a company that is ambitious and plays in a high growth environment

HEMANT MEHTA CPA (Canada), ACCA (UK)
